merer: adjective. comparative form of mere: more mere
moo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Monom.

mor: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Moro.
more: determiner. comparative degree of many: in greater number.
moreover: adverb. In addition to what has been said.
morro: noun. A round hill or point of land.
move: verb. To change place or posture; to go, in any manner, from one place or position to another.
mover: noun. Someone who or something that moves.
omer: noun. A former small Hebrew unit of dry volume equal to about 2.3 L or 2.1 quarts.

ormer: noun. An abalone or sea-ear, particularly Haliotis tuberculata, common in the Channel Islands.

rem: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Remo.

rev: verb. To increase the speed of a motor, or to operate at a higher speed.

romeo: noun. Alternative letter-case form of Romeo of the ICAO / NATO radiotelephony alphabet.
room: noun. An opportunity or scope (to do something).
roomer: noun. A person who rents a room.

vee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter V / v.
veer: verb. To let out (a sail-line), to allow (a sheet) to run out.
voe: noun. A narrow sea inlet, particularly in the Shetland Islands, similar to a fjord.
vomer: noun. The vomer bone; the small thin bone that forms part of the septum between the nostrils.
vroom: interjection. The sound of an engine, especially when it is revving up.
emo: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Emok.
emove: verb. To stir or arouse emotion in (someone); to cause to feel emotion.
erme: verb. To grieve; to feel sad.
evoe: interjection. Alternative form of euoi (“Bacchic cry”).
mee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mengen.
mero: noun. Any of several large groupers of warm seas.
moe: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Montagnais.
oom: noun. An older man, especially an uncle. (Frequently as a respectful form of address.)
oor: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Oorlams.
oreo: noun. Alternative letter-case form of Oreo.
reem: noun. A large horned animal in ancient Hebrew literature, variously identified with the wild ox or aurochs (Bos primigenius), the Arabian

roo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Rotokas.
vor: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Voro.
